_meta.description: Patient-completed outcome measures (PROMs) for the in-clinic kiosk (Phase 4). Each PROM defines items (numeric `scale`, choice `options`, or `yesno`), optional `reverse`/`subscale`, a deterministic `scoring` shape, and interpretation `bands`. Scoring is server-side (services/prom-scoring.js), never the LLM. Seed ONLY license-safe instruments; `license` records the basis. ⚠ Item wording AUTHORED from the standard published forms — VERIFY against the official instrument before go-live.

scoring.average: Mean of items (NPRS) or clinician-entered activities (PSFS).
scoring.subscales: Sum per `subscale` (× optional multiplier), each banded (DASS-21, PROMIS-10).

proms.name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
proms.patientIntro: Please rate your pain on a scale from 0 to 10.

items.text: What is your pain right now?
On average over the past 7 days, how bad has your pain been?
At its worst over the past 7 days, how bad has your pain been?

proms.name: Patient-Specific Functional Scale
proms.license: Free for clinical use (Stratford et al. 1995).
proms.patientIntro: For each activity, rate how able you are to do it today.

activities.prompt: Activity the patient finds difficult (e.g. walking 20 min, climbing stairs)

proms.name: Lower Extremity Functional Scale
proms.license: Free for clinical use (Binkley et al. 1999).
proms.patientIntro: For each activity, rate how much difficulty you have doing it today.

items.text: Any of your usual work, housework or school activities
Your usual hobbies, recreational or sporting activities
Getting into or out of the bath
Walking between rooms
Putting on your shoes or socks
Squatting
Lifting an object, like a bag of groceries, from the floor
Performing light activities around your home
Performing heavy activities around your home
Getting into or out of a car
Walking 2 blocks
Walking a mile
Going up or down 10 stairs (about 1 flight)
Standing for 1 hour
Sitting for 1 hour
Running on even ground
Running on uneven ground
Making sharp turns while running fast
Hopping
Rolling over in bed

proms.name: Kessler Psychological Distress Scale (K10)
proms.patientIntro: In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel…

items.text: …tired out for no good reason?
…nervous?
…so nervous that nothing could calm you down?
…hopeless?
…restless or fidgety?
…so restless you could not sit still?
…depressed?
…that everything was an effort?
…so sad that nothing could cheer you up?
…worthless?

proms.name: Depression Anxiety Stress Scales (DASS-21)
proms.patientIntro: Over the past week, how much did each statement apply to you?

itemScale.maxLabel: Applied very much / most of the time

items.text: I found it hard to wind down
I was aware of dryness of my mouth
I couldn't seem to experience any positive feeling at all
I experienced breathing difficulty (e.g. rapid breathing, breathlessness without exertion)
I found it difficult to work up the initiative to do things
I tended to over-react to situations
I experienced trembling (e.g. in the hands)
I felt that I was using a lot of nervous energy
I was worried about situations in which I might panic and make a fool of myself
I felt that I had nothing to look forward to
I found myself getting agitated
I found it difficult to relax
I felt down-hearted and blue
I was intolerant of anything that kept me from getting on with what I was doing
I felt I was close to panic
I was unable to become enthusiastic about anything
I felt I wasn't worth much as a person
I felt that I was rather touchy
I was aware of my heart acting up without exertion (heart rate increase, missing a beat)
I felt scared without any good reason
I felt that life was meaningless

proms.name: Neck Disability Index
proms.patientIntro: For each section, choose the one statement that best describes you today.

items.text: Pain intensity

options.label: I have no pain at the moment
The pain is very mild at the moment
The pain is moderate at the moment
The pain is fairly severe at the moment
The pain is very severe at the moment
The pain is the worst imaginable at the moment

items.text: Personal care (washing, dressing, etc.)

options.label: I can look after myself normally without causing extra pain
I can look after myself normally but it causes extra pain
It is painful to look after myself and I am slow and careful
I need some help but can manage most of my personal care
I need help every day in most aspects of self care
I do not get dressed, I wash with difficulty and stay in bed

items.text: Lifting

options.label: I can lift heavy weights without extra pain
I can lift heavy weights but it gives extra pain
Pain prevents lifting heavy weights off the floor, but I can manage if conveniently placed (e.g. on a table)
Pain prevents lifting heavy weights, but I can manage light to medium weights if conveniently positioned
I can only lift very light weights
I cannot lift or carry anything

items.text: Reading

options.label: I can read as much as I want with no pain in my neck
I can read as much as I want with slight pain in my neck
I can read as much as I want with moderate pain in my neck
I can't read as much as I want because of moderate pain in my neck
I can hardly read at all because of severe pain in my neck
I cannot read at all

items.text: Headaches

options.label: I have no headaches at all
I have slight headaches which come infrequently
I have moderate headaches which come infrequently
I have moderate headaches which come frequently
I have severe headaches which come frequently
I have headaches almost all the time

items.text: Concentration

options.label: I can concentrate fully when I want to with no difficulty
I can concentrate fully when I want to with slight difficulty
I have a fair degree of difficulty concentrating when I want to
I have a lot of difficulty concentrating when I want to
I have a great deal of difficulty concentrating when I want to
I cannot concentrate at all

items.text: Work

options.label: I can do as much work as I want to
I can only do my usual work, but no more
I can do most of my usual work, but no more
I cannot do my usual work
I can hardly do any work at all
I can't do any work at all

items.text: Driving

options.label: I can drive my car without any neck pain
I can drive my car as long as I want with slight pain in my neck
I can drive my car as long as I want with moderate pain in my neck
I can't drive my car as long as I want because of moderate pain in my neck
I can hardly drive at all because of severe pain in my neck
I can't drive my car at all

items.text: Sleeping

options.label: I have no trouble sleeping
My sleep is slightly disturbed (less than 1 hr sleepless)
My sleep is mildly disturbed (1–2 hrs sleepless)
My sleep is moderately disturbed (2–3 hrs sleepless)
My sleep is greatly disturbed (3–5 hrs sleepless)
My sleep is completely disturbed (5–7 hrs sleepless)

items.text: Recreation

options.label: I am able to engage in all my recreation activities with no neck pain at all
I am able to engage in all my recreation activities with some pain in my neck
I am able to engage in most, but not all, of my usual recreation activities because of neck pain
I am able to engage in a few of my usual recreation activities because of neck pain
I can hardly do any recreation activities because of neck pain
I can't do any recreation activities at all

proms.name: Oswestry Disability Index
proms.license: © Fairbank/Mapi Research Trust. Free for non-funded academic research and individual clinical practice; other (incl. commercial/electronic) uses may require a licence — confirm before go-live.
proms.patientIntro: For each section, choose the one statement that best describes you today.

items.text: Pain intensity

options.label: I have no pain at the moment
The pain is very mild at the moment
The pain is moderate at the moment
The pain is fairly severe at the moment
The pain is very severe at the moment
The pain is the worst imaginable at the moment

items.text: Personal care (washing, dressing, etc.)

options.label: I can look after myself normally without causing extra pain
I can look after myself normally but it causes extra pain
It is painful to look after myself and I am slow and careful
I need some help but manage most of my personal care
I need help every day in most aspects of personal care
I do not get dressed, I wash with difficulty and stay in bed

items.text: Lifting

options.label: I can lift heavy weights without increased pain
I can lift heavy weights but it causes increased pain
Pain prevents lifting heavy weights off the floor, but I can manage if conveniently positioned (e.g. on a table)
Pain prevents lifting heavy weights off the floor, but I can manage light to medium weights if conveniently positioned
I can lift only very light weights
I cannot lift or carry anything at all

items.text: Walking

options.label: Pain does not prevent me walking any distance
Pain prevents me from walking more than 1 mile
Pain prevents me from walking more than 1/2 mile
Pain prevents me from walking more than 100 yards
I can only walk using a stick or crutches
I am in bed most of the time and have to crawl to the toilet

items.text: Sitting

options.label: I can sit in any chair as long as I like
I can sit in my favorite chair as long as I like
Pain prevents me from sitting for more than 1 hour
Pain prevents me from sitting for more than 1/2 hour
Pain prevents me from sitting for more than 10 minutes
Pain prevents me from sitting at all

items.text: Standing

options.label: I can stand as long as I want without increased pain
I can stand as long as I want but my pain increases with time
Pain prevents me from standing more than 1 hour
Pain prevents me from standing more than 1/2 hour
Pain prevents me from standing more than 10 minutes
Pain prevents me from standing at all

items.text: Sleeping

options.label: My sleep is not disturbed by pain
My sleep is occasionally disturbed by pain
Because of pain, my sleep is only 3/4 of my normal amount
Because of pain, my sleep is only 1/2 of my normal amount
Because of pain, my sleep is only 1/4 of my normal amount
Pain prevents me from sleeping at all

items.text: Social life

options.label: My social life is normal and does not increase my pain
My social life is normal but it increases my level of pain
Pain has no significant effect on my social life apart from limiting more energetic activities (e.g. sport)
Pain has restricted my social life and I do not go out as often
Pain has restricted my social life to my home
I have no social life because of pain

items.text: Travelling

options.label: I get no increased pain when travelling
I get some pain while travelling but none of my usual forms of travel make it any worse
I get increased pain while travelling but it does not cause me to seek alternative forms of travel
I get increased pain while travelling which causes me to seek alternative forms of travel
Pain restricts all forms of travel except that done while lying down
Pain restricts all forms of travel

items.text: Sex life (if applicable)

options.label: My sex life is normal and does not increase my pain
My sex life is normal but increases my level of pain
My sex life is nearly normal but is very painful
My sex life is severely restricted by pain
My sex life is nearly absent because of pain
Pain prevents any sex life at all

proms.name: Upper Extremity Functional Index
proms.license: Free for clinical use (Stratford et al. 2001).
proms.patientIntro: For each activity, rate how much difficulty you have doing it today.

items.text: Your usual work, housework or school activities
Your usual hobbies, recreational or sporting activities
Lifting a bag of groceries to waist level
Lifting a bag of groceries above your head
Grooming your hair
Pushing up on your hands (e.g. from a bathtub or chair)
Preparing food (e.g. peeling, cutting)
Driving
Vacuuming, sweeping or raking
Dressing
Doing up buttons
Using tools or appliances
Opening doors
Cleaning
Tying or lacing shoes
Sleeping
Laundering clothes (e.g. washing, ironing, folding)
Opening a jar
Throwing a ball
Carrying a small suitcase with your affected limb

proms.name: Roland-Morris Disability Questionnaire
proms.patientIntro: For each statement, tap Yes if it describes you today because of your back, otherwise No.

items.text: I stay at home most of the time because of my back
I change position frequently to try to get my back comfortable
I walk more slowly than usual because of my back
Because of my back, I am not doing any jobs that I usually do around the house
Because of my back, I use a handrail to get upstairs
Because of my back, I lie down to rest more often
Because of my back, I have to hold on to something to get out of an easy chair
Because of my back, I try to get other people to do things for me
I get dressed more slowly than usual because of my back
I only stand up for short periods of time because of my back
Because of my back, I try not to bend or kneel down
I find it difficult to get out of a chair because of my back
My back is painful almost all the time
I find it difficult to turn over in bed because of my back
My appetite is not very good because of my back pain
I have trouble putting on my socks (or stockings) because of the pain in my back
I only walk short distances because of my back pain
I sleep less well because of my back
Because of my back pain, I get dressed with help from someone else
I sit down for most of the day because of my back
I avoid heavy jobs around the house because of my back
Because of my back pain, I am more irritable and bad tempered with people than usual
Because of my back, I go upstairs more slowly than usual
I stay in bed most of the time because of my back

proms.name: Örebro Musculoskeletal Pain Screening Questionnaire (Short Form)
proms.license: Free for clinical use (Linton, Nicholas & MacDonald 2011).
proms.patientIntro: These questions help us understand your pain and how it affects you.

items.text: How long have you had your current pain problem?

options.label: 0–1 weeks
1–2 weeks
3–4 weeks
4–5 weeks
6–8 weeks
9–11 weeks
3–6 months
6–9 months
9–12 months
Over 1 year

items.text: How would you rate the pain you have had during the past week?

scale.maxLabel: Pain as bad as it could be

items.text: I can do light work for an hour.

scale.minLabel: Can't do it because of the pain
scale.maxLabel: Can do it without pain being a problem

items.text: I can sleep at night.

scale.minLabel: Can't do it because of the pain
scale.maxLabel: Can do it without pain being a problem

items.text: How tense or anxious have you felt in the past week?

scale.maxLabel: As tense and anxious as I've ever felt

items.text: How much have you been bothered by feeling depressed in the past week?
In your view, how large is the risk that your current pain may become persistent?
In your estimation, what are the chances you will be working your normal duties in 3 months?
An increase in pain is an indication that I should stop what I'm doing until the pain decreases.
I should not do my normal work with my present pain.
